mnemonics: CoAP is Constrained, MQTT is for Messaging
keyPoints: CoAP is a web transfer protocol for constrained nodes and networks, designed for machine-to-machine applications.
MQTT is a lightweight publish/subscribe messaging protocol designed for connections with remote locations where network bandwidth is limited.
HTTP is not always suitable for IoT due to its high overhead; protocols like CoAP and MQTT are optimized for low-power, lossy networks.
commonMistakes: Assuming HTTP is the best protocol for all IoT applications, ignoring its high overhead.
Confusing MQTT (publish/subscribe) with CoAP (request/response) and using them in the wrong scenario.
